div1.entryFree: Bitterly, adv., 1) in a manner expressing poignant grief: wept b. Gentl. IV, 4, 176. cried b. Rom. I, 3, 54. -- 2) with acrimony: speak b. Meas. V, 36. R3 III, 7, 142. R3 III, 7, 142 IV, 4, 180. H8 I, 2, 24. -- 3) sharply: the north-east wind blew b. against our faces, R2 I, 4, 7. -- 4) calamitously: some consequence yet hanging in the stars shall b. begin his fearful date, Rom. I, 4, 108.
